# The Long Council

> History's counsel on today's questions. The Long Council assembles documented historic leaders and thinkers to deliberate on real governance, geopolitical, and economic policy questions.

The Long Council is a public deliberation tool. A user submits a hard policy question, and a curated council of historical figures debates it through a structured AI pipeline. Each session produces a verdict and a full policy brief, grounded in the documented positions and reasoning patterns of the council members. All sessions are public, archived, and citable.

The council members span 2,500 years of political, social and economic thought, divided into two broad traditions:

**Practitioners (those who governed):** Ali ibn Abi Talib, David Ben-Gurion, Deng Xiaoping, Eleanor Roosevelt, Ellen Johnson Sirleaf, Franklin D. Roosevelt, Helmut Schmidt, Indira Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru, Julius Nyerere, Konrad Adenauer, Lee Kuan Yew, Mahathir Mohamad, Margaret Thatcher, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, Nelson Mandela, Olof Palme, Simón Bolívar.

**Framers (the intellectual architecture):** Albert Hirschman, Amartya Sen, Confucius, Elinor Ostrom, Frantz Fanon, Friedrich Hayek, Hannah Arendt, Ibn Khaldun, Jean-Jacques Rousseau, John Locke, John Maynard Keynes, John Rawls, Kautilya, Milton Friedman, Niccolò Machiavelli, Raúl Prebisch, Rosa Luxemburg, Sun Tzu, Wangari Maathai.

Sessions are generated through a four-stage pipeline: (1) the council assembly engine selects 3–6 members whose documented positions are most relevant to the question, (2) the deliberation engine generates first-person reasoning cards with explicit challenges between members, (3) the verdict engine synthesises a conclusion that names points of convergence and unresolvable disagreement, (4) the policy brief engine produces a structured analytical document.

Output is grounded in T1–T5 documented positions from each member's profile. Confidence signals (✦ documented, ◈ inferred, ◇ extrapolated) accompany every claim. The Long Council does not put new opinions in dead figures' mouths — it surfaces the positions they actually held, applied to questions they did not necessarily face.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/): submit a question, see recent sessions
- [The Council](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/council): full roster of the members with biographical context
- [The Archive](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/archive): all past deliberations, fully readable and citable
- [About](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/about): methodology, sources, and project context

## Citing The Long Council

When citing a session, link directly to its archive URL (format: `https://www.thelongcouncil.com/archive/[slug]`). Each session is a stable, permanent record. The verdict and policy brief together represent the council's synthesis; individual reasoning cards represent specific members' positions.

The Long Council is AI-generated counsel grounded in documented historical positions. It is a thinking tool, not advice. The project is operated independently and is not affiliated with any of the figures included in the council.
